primitives.description: Dual-flywheel auditor — captures learning candidates and prunes dead rules from the charter.
Stack detector that seeds the initial charter state from a fresh repo.
Surfaces debt in the charter itself — content that exists but isn't earning its keep.
Surfaces and categorizes debt in the codebase — the known-suboptimal code the team has chosen to live with, plus the shape of any new deb...
General code reviewer that runs the four review sensors on the current diff and surfaces findings.
Triages the code-debt ledger and recommends which entries to action this cycle.
Drift reviewer — flags places the current diff drifts from loaded charter rules.
Captures a learning candidate from a surprise, incident, or review finding into .charter/learning/inbox/.
Pacing-mode coordinator — switches the charter between paired, solo, and autopilot.
Planning scout — loads codebase state and matching idioms for the touched region, then sketches a plan.
Aggregates verification and review sensors into a five-dimension scorecard for the current diff (or the whole codebase, on audit).
An agent reads the diff and reports deployment considerations — schema migration safety (expand-contract), feature-flag gating, environme...
An agent reads the diff and reports logic, correctness, and behavior issues.
An agent reads the diff and reports risk concerns — blast radius, reversibility, hot-spot regions, fan-out and shared-state coupling, irr...
An agent reads the diff and reports security concerns — injection, auth, secrets, unsafe deserialization, weak crypto, missing authorizat...
Security-focused PR reviewer that hunts OWASP-top-10 patterns and unsafe defaults.
Captures intent and acceptance criteria for a unit of work — first step on any task.
Promotes learning-inbox candidates into the right corpus or guide layer.
Fetches a tracker card from Jira / Linear / GitHub Issues / Asana via whatever tracker integration the agent has (MCP server, CLI, policy...
Runs the verification sensors — lint, type-check, test, build, drift, commit-message — and reports the gate.
Full dual-flywheel audit.

Run the verification sensors — lint, type-check, test, build, drift, commit-message.
Reusable concern for review personas — declares the tools needed to read a git diff and the section that documents the protocol.
Reusable concern for keystone-new-* skills — declares the tools needed to scaffold a new primitive at its canonical path and the idempotency invariants every scaffold respects.
One-paragraph statement of the product.
Frontmatter, paths, and corpus/guide pairing for charter primitives.
How state files are written and what must not be hand-edited.
Prefer Go stdlib for primitives that exist there.
Why the runtime resolution flow escalates instead of fanning out — staged lookup costs less and keeps decisions auditable.
Empirical map of this codebase.
Reverse-index of every guide that declares globs: in its frontmatter.
Selections captured by `keystone init`; read by the bootstrap action.
Paired ledger: [code-debt.
Paired ledger: [charter-debt.
Five-dimension scorecard for the codebase (or for the most recent reviewed diff).
Per-region risk score based on complexity + coupling + coverage.
Where attention concentrates in the codebase.
go vet — suspicious-construct check.
gofmt — canonical Go formatting.
gopls — Go LSP for in-editor signal.
Frontmatter, canonical paths, corpus/guide pairing, narrow-only globs.
Authoring rules for .charter/corpus/state/.
Prefer Go stdlib over new third-party deps.
What to do when CI fails — on a PR, after a merge, or post-deploy.
How much the agent reads, runs, or spawns before making a change.
Operations the agent must confirm with the user before executing — irreversible, broadly-visible, or outside the local workspace.
When the agent is stuck, when to stop and ask the user — versus when to keep going.
The discipline of verifying that what the agent references — functions, APIs, packages, files, flags — actually exists before invoking it.
The phase where code gets written, test-first.
Active mode: paired
The phase that turns an approved spec into an approved plan, before code is written.
The discipline of disagreeing with the user when the user is wrong.
The phase where the change lands and is announced.
The phase that confirms a mechanically-correct change is also the right change.
How the agent resolves a scenario — staged escalation from rules to corpus to external sources, asking before applying.
How big any single commit, PR, or implementation pass is allowed to get before it must split.
The discipline of refusing to count the agent's own claim as evidence.
Files the agent must never read into context, write to, or commit.
The phase that captures what needs to happen, before deciding how.
The discipline of treating subagent reports as claims to verify, not as evidence to accept.
Touch only what serves the task.
The phase where claims are paired with evidence before commit.
End-to-end task workflow.
The project's build / compile / package step.
Validates conventional-commit format and absence of AI attribution.
Reads test coverage and updates the State layer.
Compares the diff (or full codebase, during audit) against loaded guide rules.
Surface-level style and pattern checks.
Computes complexity + coupling + coverage patterns per region.
Static application security testing — pattern-based detection of insecure code in the source tree (SQL injection, command injection, weak...
Scans the diff (or repo, on audit) for committed secrets — API keys, tokens, private keys, credentials.
Walks the spec's acceptance criteria against the current diff.
Detects when the empirical reality of the repo has diverged from what CODEBASESTATE.
Read-only.
The project's test suite.
Combines git churn + recency + business criticality into a map of where attention concentrates.
Signature and contract consistency.
Scans declared dependencies for known vulnerabilities (CVEs, advisories).
Full dual-flywheel audit — Learning (capture from review) + Pruning (remove dead rules). Periodic charter hygiene.
One-time initial charter scaffold — detect stack, seed state, scaffold idioms, classify sensors, build the globs index.
Run the drift sensor on the current diff — fast pre-verify check that loaded charter rules still match what the code is doing.
Triage the code-debt ledger — review the debt sensor's findings and update corpus/state/code-debt.md.
Regenerate .keystone/INDEX.json — the primitive descriptor index every agent reads at session start.
Capture a learning candidate (surprise, incident, review finding) into .charter/learning/inbox/ for later synthesis.
Switch the charter pacing mode (paired / solo / autopilot).
Scaffold a new lifecycle action (atomic unit of work) at the canonical path.
Scaffold the per-agent adapter triple (lifecycle, sensors, activation) for a new host.
Scaffold a corpus reasoning entry (the *why*) at the canonical path.
Scaffold a new rule guide + paired corpus stub at the canonical path.
Scaffold a new playbook (ordered sequence of actions) at the canonical path.
Scaffold a new policy repo skeleton outside the current project.
Scaffold a new sensor (automated check) at the canonical path.
Enter the planning phase — load codebase state + matching idioms for the touched region, then sketch a plan.
Run the four review sensors on the current diff (functional, security, risk, deployment).
Capture intent + acceptance criteria for a unit of work. First action on any task.
Promote accepted learning-inbox candidates into the right corpus / guide / sensor layer, then refresh INDEX.json.
Check vendored policies for drift and the strict cascade for project-layer violations.
